summary: Pure Telegram API functions for channel resolution and message fetching, plus the Message domain type that is the shared data contract consumed by source subsystems

requires: Live *tg.Client (must be called within client.go Run callback)
Valid channel username for ResolveChannel; resolved *tg.InputChannel for FetchMessages
guarantees: Returns only text-bearing messages — media-only, service, and empty messages are silently dropped
Message.Timestamp is always UTC-normalized from Unix epoch

effects: Telegram API network calls: ContactsResolveUsername, MessagesGetHistory

observations.text: extractMessages silently discards MessageEmpty, MessageService, and any message with empty .Message text. Callers receive fewer items than the requested limit with no indication of how many were dropped.
debug bool causes verbose raw message dumps to os.Stdout (including %#v of full gotd structs). If true in production, this leaks message content and floods logs with unstructured output.

HINT MATCH — Message struct (ID, Text, Timestamp) is the shared type consumed by source/telegram and referenced in tests for source/hackernews, source/rss, source/reddit. This file is the de-facto domain model for messages across all source subsystems.
ResolveChannel strips a leading '@' from the username, so callers can pass either '@channelname' or 'channelname' interchangeably

FetchMessages handles three polymorphic gotd response types (MessagesMessages, MessagesMessagesSlice, MessagesChannelMessages) and returns an error on any unrecognized type — forward-compatible but will break on new gotd response variants
